mechanism_narrative: A multi-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals contracting residential investment and household demand that precedes broader economic slowdown, causing S&P 500 forward returns to be negative over the next quarter as earnings expectations reprice downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1474k units, down substantially from 2005-2006 peak levels above 2000k
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST trailing 6-month change and 60-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or SPX posts positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months when HOUST falls for 3+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains above 5% for an extended period while economic indicators are decelerating, the restrictive policy stance suppresses credit availability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month until the Fed signals easing.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS>5% restrictive-plateau periods are not significantly different from the full-sample average (t-test p>0.10), or median return is positive across triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of falling housing starts (HOUST) and a restrictive federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS staying elevated) compounds financial stress in the housing-credit channel, amplifying negative wealth effects and credit tightening beyond either signal alone, driving S&P 500 returns lower over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST declining while FEDFUNDS held at 5.25%
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns under joint trigger are not more negative than returns under either single-signal trigger alone (interaction effect not statistically distinguishable, p>0.10), rejecting incremental interaction value.

mechanism_narrative: Continued payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) reflects robust consumer income and spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5%, PAYEMS at 138038k reflecting tight labor market
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth during periods when UNRATE is below 5%, across any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in headline CPI (CPIAUCSL) above trend raises inflation expectations and the probability of continued or renewed Fed tightening, causing an immediate negative repricing of equities over the following week as discount rate expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 207.387 with FEDFUNDS held at 5.25% suggesting inflation vigilance
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following CPI upside surprises above the trigger threshold are not significantly negative (p>0.10) across a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and business activity, which is quickly absorbed into short-horizon equity pricing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.6889 continuing an expansionary trend
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 1-month INDPRO change and 5-day forward SPX return is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions when retail sales (RSAFS) continue to grow despite tightening monetary policy, resilient consumer spending sustains corporate revenue growth expectations and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month, but this relationship weakens or reverses once the expansion matures into contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 373946 million dollars showing continued monthly growth despite high FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: The positive relationship between RSAFS growth and forward SPX returns flips sign or loses significance (p>0.10) once GDPC1 growth decelerates below 1% annualized, invalidating the late-cycle resilience premise.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime where unemployment (UNRATE) is at cyclical lows while housing starts (HOUST) are falling sharply, the divergence between a lagging labor market and a leading demand indicator signals an approaching turning point, causing S&P 500 forward returns to be negative over the next quarter as the labor market eventually catches down to weakening housing-driven demand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% while HOUST at 1474k is well below prior cyclical peak
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following the joint UNRATE-low/HOUST-falling trigger are not significantly more negative than unconditional 60-day returns (p>0.10) across a 3-year rolling walk-forward test, or the divergence resolves via housing recovery rather than labor deterioration in more than half of historical instances.
